clinical_history: This ECG from a 65 year old woman shows which ONE of the following rhythms?
diagnosis: The ECG, perhaps surprisingly at first glance, shows sinus rhythm with a distinct artifact that simulates an atrial tachyarrhythmia. Careful attention reveals clear sinus P waves in leads III and V1. The oscillatory baseline fluctuations in others leads, at a "rate" of about 300 cycles/min, is typical of tremor artifact with Parkinson's disease. Note that the artifact is least apparent in lead III (LL-LA), implying that the tremor is most marked in the right hand, accounting for the non-cardiac oscillations being most evident in lead I (LA-RA) and II (LL-RA). There are non-diagnostic Q waves in leads III and aVF so that a prior inferior wall MI cannot be excluded. The biphasic P in V1 with a broad negative component is consistent with left atrial abnormality. (see: Littmann L. Electrocardiographic artifact. J Electrocardiol. 2021;64:23-9.)
